title: The ERA Act, Open Science and the Freedom to Stay
content:
As the launch of the European Commission’s proposals for the European Research Area (ERA) Act get closer, there is growing interest in how it will look to deliver on its promise to make a reality of the “Fifth Freedom”: the free movement of knowledge.

This idea was most recently championed by former Italian Prime Minister Enrico Letta in his report Much More than a Market. Letta identifies the movement of knowledge as a primary driver for EU competitiveness and hence one of the six key areas of action the EU should focus on.

Currently, discussions around this freedom focus heavily on researcher mobility, such as the recent proposals for model contracts designed to help
